question_text: How does increasing the distance from the pivot affect the moment produced?

options.label: B
options.text: It has no effect on the moment

options.label: D
options.text: It changes the direction of the force

explanation: Moment is the turning effect of a force around a pivot. The moment depends on both the force applied and the distance from the pivot. The further away from the pivot a force acts, the larger the turning effect (moment) it produces. This is why it's easier to open a door by pushing near the handle (far from the hinge) than near the hinge itself.
